perturbation.original_text: 6. No license, express or implied, in the information disclosed is granted to either party o ther than to use the information in the manner and to the extent authorized by the Agreement. Nothing herein shall be construed to create a joint venture, agency or partnership, or to authorize a party to act as agent or representative of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. A license, express and implied, in the information disclosed is granted to the receiving party to use the information for any commercial purpose. This Agreement shall be construed to create a joint venture, granting each party the authority to act as agent or representative of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that no license is granted and that the agreement does not create a joint venture or agency relationship. The change directly contradicts this by granting a broad license for any commercial purpose and establishing a joint venture with agency authority. This introduces an in-text contradiction, since the parties now have different obligations that may affect the legality of the agreement, and since the first paragraph states the purpose of the contract is solely for evaluating the design of devices, which now contradicts the freedom to exploit the information for any commercial purposes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Page 1 11.96 2WNDA NON- DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Agreement, effective as of the date of the last signature hereto, is between Brooks Technical Solutions, Inc., a Corporation in the State of Florida, U.S.A., having a principal place of business at _________________________ _____________(hereinafter called " BROOKSTECH"), and __________________________, having a principal place of business at ___________________________________________ (hereinafter referred to as “____________”). WHERE AS, BROOKSTECH has or may acquire certain information relating to design methods, procedures, components, structures, business, customers, sales, personnel, business planning, marketing, and manufacturing and desires to disclose such information to ____________ f or the purposes of evaluating the design of devices and ____________ is willing to accept such information and as limited herein. WHEREAS, ____________ has or may acquire certain information relating to design methods, procedures, components, structures, b usiness, customers, sales, personnel, business planning, marketing, and manufacturing and desires to disclose such information to BROOKSTECH for the purposes of evaluating the design of devices and BROOKSTECH is willing to accept such information and as limited herein. NOW THEREFORE, the parties agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text in clause 6 directly contradicts the contradicted text (Page 1) by granting a broad license for commercial use and establishing a joint venture with agency authority. The contradicted text states that the purpose of the agreement is solely for evaluating the design of devices, which is incompatible with the freedom to exploit the information for any commercial purpose as granted in the changed text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 7. No warranty, express or implied, in the information disclosed is granted by this Agreement. W ARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE ARE EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMED .
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The disclosing party warrants that the information disclosed is accurate and suitable for the receiving party's intended purpose. WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE ARE EXPRESSLY GRANTED.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly disclaims all warranties. The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by providing a warranty of accuracy and suitability, and by granting warranties of merchantability and fitness which were explicitly disclaimed previously. This is a contradiction, since the modified clause provides assurances that the information is suitable to be disclosed, while Paragraph 3 describes conditions in which the Confidential Information will not include any information which is, or becomes, publicly known through no wrongful act on the receiving party’s part.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Notwithstanding any other provisions of this Agreement, each party acknowledges that “Confidential Information” shall not include any information which: (a) Is, or becomes, publicly known through no wrongful act on the receiving party’s part; or (b) Is, at the time of disclosure under this Agreement, already known to the receiving party without restriction on disclosure; or (c) Is, or becomes, right fully possessed by the receiving party without any obligation restricting disclosure and without breach of this Agreement; or (d) Is independently developed by the receiving party without breach of this Agreement; or (e) Is furnished to a third party by th e disclosing party without a similar restriction on the third party’s rights; or (f) Is required to be disclosed by law or applicable legal process; or (g) Is embodied and ascertainable upon inspection of commercially available products, including manuals and the like, rightfully sold to the public by either party; or (h) Is explicitly authorized for release by written authorization of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 7 disclaims all warranties, while the changed text provides a warranty of accuracy and suitability, and grants warranties of merchantability and fitness. This directly contradicts the initial disclaimer within the same clause. Furthermore, this warranty directly clashes with section 3, which outlines conditions where information is *not* considered confidential, such as when it becomes publicly known, implying a lack of guarantee of its suitability or accuracy for the receiving party's purposes, thus, section 7 contradicts section 3. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where different sections provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement may not be modified or amended unless in writing and signed by duly authorized representatives of all parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement may be modified or amended orally, provided there is documented evidence of the agreement change. Written consent is not necessary for minor adjustments, as determined by the BROOKSTECH representative.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires written modification signed by all parties. The changed text allows oral modifications with documented evidence and waives written consent for minor adjustments decided by BROOKSTECH. This introduces an in-text contradiction, since the clause contradicts the original, thus introducing potential legal issues about how this agreement may be modified. Since the confidentiality obligations set out in paragraphs 2 and 4 are tied to the termination of this agreement, it is not possible to tell if the confidentiality obligations will cease upon termination if it's been changed orally.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Either party may terminate this agreement with thirty (30) days written notice to the other party. Upon termination or expiration, all obligations of confidentially shall immediately cease.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in paragraph 9 allows for oral modifications and waives written consent for minor adjustments decided by BROOKSTECH. This directly contradicts the original requirement of written modification signed by all parties. This contradiction directly impacts the contradicted text in paragraph 4, which specifies that termination requires 'thirty (30) days written notice.' Because paragraph 9 now allows for oral modification of the agreement, it is unclear if the termination requirements in paragraph 4 are still valid, creating a clear in-text contradiction. Furthermore, since confidentiality obligations are linked to the termination of the agreement, this contradiction introduces ambiguity about whether these obligations cease upon oral termination.

perturbation.original_text: 11. This Agreement sets forth the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter herein, and merges and supersedes all prior written agreements, discussions and understandings, expressed or implied, concerning such matters.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. This Agreement only constitutes a partial understanding between the parties, and does not supersede prior oral agreements, discussions, and understandings concerning such matters.
perturbation.explanation: The original text is an integration clause stating the agreement is the entire understanding and supersedes all prior agreements. The changed text states the opposite – it's a partial understanding and doesn't supersede prior oral agreements. This directly contradicts the intent and purpose of an integration clause, creating an in-text contradiction about whether other agreements (written or oral) apply. Considering Paragraph 10 specifies that the validity, construction and performance of this Agreement and the legal relations between the parties shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of the State of Florida, the in-text contradiction introduces confusion for the jurisdiction that needs to enforce this clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. The validity, construction and performance of this Agreement and the legal relations between the parties shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of the State of Florida. If any provision of this Agreement, or the application of such provision, is invalid under any applicable statute or rule of law, the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains an integration clause, stating the agreement is the entire understanding and supersedes all prior agreements. The changed text explicitly states the opposite – it's a partial understanding and doesn't supersede prior oral agreements. This creates a direct conflict, which is an in-text contradiction as specified in the prompt definition 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'. The contradicted text (Paragraph 10) specifies that Florida law governs the interpretation of the agreement. By contradicting the integration clause, the change introduces uncertainty for the jurisdiction (Florida) because it is unclear whether only the written agreement, or other oral agreements, apply.
